piec
  • Hysteresis Loop
  • View page source

Hysteresis Loop

A ferroelectric hysteresis loop (P-E or P-V loop) characterizes the non-linear relationship between electric polarization (P) and applied electric field (E) in ferroelectric materials. It is the primary measurement for confirming ferroelectric switching behavior and extracting key material parameters.

Background

Applying a triangular voltage sweep to a ferroelectric capacitor drives polarization switching. The resulting P-E loop encodes:

  • Remanent polarization (P_r) — polarization remaining when the field returns to zero, indicating the material’s ability to retain a polarized state.

  • Coercive field (E_c or V_c) — the reverse field required to bring polarization to zero, related to the switching threshold.

  • Saturation polarization (P_s) — maximum polarization under a large applied field.

The shape of the loop reflects ferroelectric quality, domain structure, and suitability for memory applications.

Required instruments

  • AWG — outputs the triangular voltage waveform (e.g., Keysight81150a)

  • Oscilloscope — captures the voltage response across the series resistor (e.g., KeysightDSOX3024A)

Example

from piec.drivers.keysight81150a import Keysight81150a
from piec.drivers.keysightdsox3024a import KeysightDSOX3024A
from piec.measurement import HysteresisLoop

awg = Keysight81150a('GPIB0::8::INSTR')
osc = KeysightDSOX3024A('GPIB0::7::INSTR')

experiment = HysteresisLoop(
    awg=awg,
    osc=osc,
    frequency=1000.0,  # Hz
    amplitude=5.0,     # Volts
    n_cycles=3,
    v_div=0.5,         # Oscilloscope V/div for response channel
    area=1.0e-9,       # Device area in m² (for polarization calculation)
    save_dir='./data',
    show_plots=True,
    save_plots=True,
)

experiment.run_experiment()

Key parameters

  • frequency (float) — frequency of the triangular excitation waveform in Hz.

  • amplitude (float) — peak voltage amplitude in Volts.

  • n_cycles (int) — number of triangular waveform cycles to capture.

  • area (float) — device area in m², used to convert charge to polarization (µC/cm²).

  • save_dir (str) — directory for saved data and plots.

  • auto_timeshift (bool) — if True, automatically corrects the time offset between the applied voltage and current response.

Output

After run_experiment() completes, the following are saved to save_dir:

  • Raw data file — time-series of applied voltage and measured current.

  • P-V loop plot — polarization (µC/cm²) vs. applied voltage.

  • I-V loop plot — current vs. applied voltage.

  • Time-trace plots — polarization and voltage as functions of time.

API reference

  • HysteresisLoop

  • process_raw_hyst()
